instruction: Fix the swagger redirect functionality in the `swagger_redirects()` function in R/ui.R. When `apiPath` is configured (e.g., `/api/v1`), the redirect from `/__swagger__/` to `/__docs__/` was using absolute paths which broke documentation access when APIs are mounted at subpaths. The fix should: (1) Include the apiPath prefix in the redirect keys (e.g., `/api/v1/__swagger__/`), and (2) Use relative path targets (`../__docs__/`) instead of absolute paths so redirects work correctly regardless of apiPath configuration.
context: The `swagger_redirects()` function generates redirect routes for legacy swagger documentation paths. It should return a named list where names are the redirect source paths and values contain the target route and handler function. The function uses `get_option_or_env("plumber.apiPath", "")` to get the API path prefix. The redirects should return 301 status codes with the Location header set to the redirect target.

fail_to_pass: swagger_redirects returns correct redirect structure with default apiPath
swagger redirect handler returns 301 response with relative path
swagger redirect handler works with apiPath
swagger redirects work on mounted router with apiPath
swagger redirects work with nested apiPath (e.g., /api/v1/myapp)
swagger redirects respect environment variable for apiPath
swagger redirects handle empty apiPath correctly
multiple swagger redirects on same router work independently
pass_to_pass: swagger_redirects returns empty list when plumber.legacyRedirects = FALSE
swagger redirects are mounted on plumber router
swagger redirect overwrite warning appears when route exists

key_changes: Change redirect targets from absolute paths (e.g., `/api/v1/__docs__/`) to relative paths (e.g., `../__docs__/`)
Include apiPath prefix in redirect keys: `paste0(path, "/__swagger__/")` instead of just `/__swagger__/`
The swagger_base variable should be constructed as `paste0(path, "/__swagger__/")`
Both redirect entries (`swagger_base` and `swagger_base + "index.html"`) should use `../__docs__/` relative targets
potential_pitfalls: Using absolute paths instead of relative paths will break when apiPath is set
Forgetting to include apiPath in the redirect keys will cause routes to not match
Not handling the case when apiPath is empty string vs NULL
The relative path `../__docs__/` works because `__swagger__` and `__docs__` are siblings at the same level under apiPath
